name: Blazor WebAssembly
description: Microsoft's framework for building interactive web UIs using C# instead of JavaScript, running .NET in the browser via WebAssembly

description.value: Build client web apps with C# using WebAssembly

repository.comment: Blazor is part of the ASP.NET Core repository

initial-release.comment: First public preview released March 2018; Blazor WebAssembly GA in May 2020

github-stars.comment: Stars for the ASP.NET Core repository which contains Blazor

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained by Microsoft as part of .NET

reactivity-model.comment: Blazor uses a component lifecycle with re-rendering triggered by StateHasChanged; similar to dirty checking

component-model.comment: Components are C# classes with Razor syntax (.razor files)

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in component state with StateHasChanged; cascading parameters for hierarchy

opinionatedness.comment: Part of .NET ecosystem with established patterns and conventions

backward-compatibility.comment: Microsoft maintains strong backward compatibility within major .NET versions

compiles-to.comment: Blazor WebAssembly runs the .NET runtime in the browser via WebAssembly

typescript-support.comment: C# is the primary language; TypeScript not applicable

template-syntax.comment: Uses Razor syntax - HTML with embedded C# using @ directives

styling-approach.comment: Supports CSS isolation (scoped CSS) via .razor.css files

wasm-interop.comment: JS interop through IJSRuntime for calling JavaScript from C# and vice versa

reactive-primitives.comment: No built-in signals/observables; uses component state with manual StateHasChanged calls

computed-values.comment: C# properties can serve as computed values but no reactive computed primitives

component-slots.comment: ChildContent and RenderFragment for content projection

teleport-portal.comment: No built-in portal/teleport; requires JS interop or third-party solutions

suspense.comment: No Suspense-like boundaries; loading states handled manually

context-provide-inject.comment: CascadingValue and CascadingParameter for dependency injection down the tree

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: ElementReference and JS interop for direct DOM access

official-router.comment: Built-in routing with @page directive and NavigationManager

route-guards.comment: AuthorizeRouteView and navigation interception with LocationChanged

scroll-restoration.comment: Requires manual implementation or JS interop

built-in-state.comment: Component state via fields/properties and StateHasChanged

built-in-global-state.comment: No official global state library; uses DI services or third-party like Fluxor

global-state-library.comment: No official library; community options include Fluxor and Blazor-State

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No browser devtools extension for state inspection; use Visual Studio debugging

state-persistence.comment: Requires manual implementation with localStorage/sessionStorage via JS interop

time-travel-debugging.comment: Not available; third-party libraries like Fluxor may provide this

official-cli.comment: dotnet CLI with 'dotnet new blazorwasm' template

hot-module-replacement.comment: Hot Reload supported in .NET 6+ for development

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser extension; debugging done through Visual Studio

vs-code-extension.comment: C# Dev Kit and Razor extensions for VS Code; full support in Visual Studio

error-messages.comment: Error messages are generally helpful with stack traces but can be cryptic for WASM-specific issues

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent Microsoft documentation with tutorials, guides, and API reference

playground.comment: Third-party playground by Telerik; no official playground

bundle-size-core.comment: ~2MB minimum with Brotli compression; includes .NET runtime. Uncompressed can be 5.7MB+

bundle-size-full.comment: ~5MB+ for a typical app; varies greatly based on features. .NET runtime must be downloaded.

virtual-dom.comment: Uses a render tree (similar concept to virtual DOM) for diffing

compile-time-optimization.comment: AOT compilation and IL trimming available

code-splitting.comment: Lazy loading of assemblies provides code splitting

lazy-components.comment: Supports lazy loading of assemblies and components

ssr-capable.comment: Blazor Server and Blazor Web App provide SSR; WASM can pre-render

partial-hydration.comment: .NET 8 introduced render modes allowing mixed static and interactive content

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Blazor WASM typically performs poorly in js-framework-benchmark due to WASM overhead; exact scores vary by version

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Blazor uses NuGet, not npm. NuGet packages have millions of total downloads but weekly stats differ from npm

component-libraries.comment: Major libraries include MudBlazor, Radzen, Syncfusion, DevExpress, Telerik, Blazorise, Ant Design Blazor, etc.

meta-framework.comment: Blazor itself is the full-stack solution; no separate meta-framework needed

discord-community-size.comment: Blazor Community Discord exists but exact size not readily available; part of larger .NET community

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Approximately 15,000+ questions tagged with 'blazor' as of 2025

job-market-demand.comment: Growing niche within .NET ecosystem; jobs exist but significantly fewer than React/Angular

official-testing-library.comment: No official testing library; bUnit is the community standard

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Selenium, and other E2E frameworks

snapshot-testing.comment: bUnit supports snapshot testing for rendered markup

mobile-framework.comment: Blazor Hybrid with .NET MAUI for iOS, Android, Mac, Windows

pwa-support.comment: PWA template available with service worker support

desktop-framework.comment: Blazor Hybrid with .NET MAUI or Electron

learning-curve.comment: Requires knowledge of C#, .NET, Razor syntax, and WASM concepts; steeper for non-.NET developers

concepts-to-learn.comment: C#, Razor syntax, components, data binding, DI, routing, lifecycle, JS interop, HTTP client, state management, authentication, deployment

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes with .NET SDK installed; first run downloads dependencies

migration-path.comment: Guides for migrating from older .NET versions and other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Blazor WASM is typically all-or-nothing; difficult to incrementally add to existing JS apps
